zh.Stanton Drew, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特斯坦頓 德魯
zh.of Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Pavy於大約1425年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托時，她的父親William Pavy37歲，母親Joan Beauflour32歲。她和Sir Richard Choke至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1460年之前，埋葬在英國薩默塞特斯坦頓 德魯。

zh-hans.Stanton Drew, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特斯坦頓 德魯
zh-hans.of Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Pavy于大约1425年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托时，她的父亲William Pavy37岁，母亲Joan Beauflour32岁。她和Sir Richard Choke至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1460年之前，埋葬在英国薩默塞特斯坦頓 德魯。

nameMeanings.name: Pavy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Monique, Octave.
French (northern, mainly Pas-de-Calais): probably a nickname, metonymic occupational name or topographic name derived from Old French pavé ‘cobblestone’, also ‘paved lane’.
French (mainly southern): variant of Pavie, habitational name from the Italian city of Pavia (Pavie in French). This surname is very rare in southern France.

variants.name: Pavey
meanings: English (southern): from the female personal name Pavia (possibly Old French pavie ‘peach’ or Pavie ‘woman from Pavia’ in Italy). The surname became confused with Paver .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Pavy was born about 1425, in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, her father, William Pavy, was 37 and her mother, Joan Beauflour, was 32. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Sir Richard Choke. She died before 1460, and was buried in Stanton Drew, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Pavy est née environ 1425, à Bristol, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, William Pavy, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Joan Beauflour, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Richard Choke. Elle est décédée avant 1460, et a été enterrée à Stanton Drew, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Pavy um 1425 in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Pavy 37 und ihre Mutter Joan Beauflour 32. Sie hatte mit Sir Richard Choke mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 1460 und wurde in Stanton Drew, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Pavy è nata il circa 1425 nel Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, William Pavy, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Joan Beauflour, aveva 32 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Sir Richard Choke. Morì prima del 1460, e fu sepolta a Stanton Drew, Somerset, England.

ja.Stanton Drew, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトスタントン ドリュー
ja.of Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストル
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Pavyが約1425年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストルで生まれた時，父William Pavyは37歳，母Joan Beauflourは32歳であった。彼女はSir Richard Chokeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1460年以前に死去し，イギリスソメルセトスタントン ドリューにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Pavy(이)가 대략 1425년 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Pavy(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Beauflour(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard Choke(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 이전 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 스탠턴 드루에 묻혔다.

mn.Stanton Drew, Somerset, England: Stanton Drew, Bro an Hañv, Английн

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Pavy nasceu aproximadamente 1425, em Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Pavy, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Joan Beauflour, tinha 32 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Sir Richard Choke. Ela faleceu antes 1460, e foi sepultada em Stanton Drew, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Pavy родилась около 1425 г. в местности бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Pavy было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Beauflour, 32. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Sir Richard Choke. Она умерла перед 1460 г. и была похоронена в местности стантон дрю, сомирсет, Англия.
